paragraph.title: Fair usage & bias
paragraph.text: A major challenge of AI is the risk of unfair bias in outcomes, where AI systems may systematically disadvantage certain groups of financial consumers. Financial service providers should implement appropriate governance and controls to identify, measure and mitigate the risk of unfair bias throughout the AI lifecycle.

connections.rationale: OECD backs the fairness stance and adds a human-agency & oversight mechanism paragraph 3.5 does not yet name.

quote.text: AI actors should implement mechanisms and safeguards, such as capacity for human agency and oversight, including to address risks arising from uses outside of intended purpose.

connections.rationale: NIST calls for a pre-deployment bias assessment across demographic groups that paragraph 3.5 does not require.

quote.text: Fairness and bias — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and results are documented, including the demographic groups and contexts assessed.

source.title: BNM Fair Treatment of Financial Consumers

connections.rationale: The fair-treatment obligation already covers non-discriminatory outcomes; 3.5 restates it for AI rather than cross-referencing.

quote.text: A financial service provider must ensure that financial consumers are treated fairly at all stages of their relationship with the financial service provider.

connections.reason: Identified as a likely peer benchmark for 3.5, but the MAS site blocks automated access. Upload the source to analyse this connection.
